title: Tokenized equities trading volume hits record $3.57B in a day
summary: Onchain trading of tokenized stocks surged to a new all-time high of $3.57 billion on Monday. This growth reflects increasing interest in crypto-based stock trading platforms.
body:
Tokenized equities trading volume reached a record $3.57 billion in a single day, according to data from The Block. This milestone was hit on Monday, marking the highest daily volume since the start of the year.

The growth in onchain equities trading has been steady, with volume increasing consistently since January. Tokenized stocks allow investors to trade traditional equities on blockchain platforms, combining the benefits of cryptocurrency markets with traditional stock trading.

This surge in volume highlights the growing interest in tokenized assets, which offer greater liquidity, 24/7 trading, and fractional ownership. For everyday investors, this means more opportunities to diversify portfolios and access global markets through crypto exchanges.

As tokenized equities gain traction, more traditional financial institutions may enter the space, potentially leading to further integration between traditional finance and blockchain technology. Investors should watch for new platforms and regulatory developments that could shape the future of this market.

Read more → https://www.theblock.co/post/401935/tokenized-equities-daily-volume-hits-all-time-high-of-3-57-billion?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

Onchain equities trading has been growing steadily since January, combining traditional stocks with blockchain benefits like 24/7 trading and fractional ownership.

This surge shows growing interest in tokenized assets. Watch for more platforms and regulatory changes in this space. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-05-20-tokenized-equities-trading-volume-hits-record-357b-in-a-day
standaloneTweet: Tokenized stocks hit a record $3.57B in daily trading volume. This means more opportunities for investors to trade traditional equities on crypto platforms.
